subheadline: Generate working applications in minutes with AI. Publish as live websites in seconds.
value_proposition: An AI-powered builder that takes you from a text prompt to a deployed web app or site in seconds, with GitHub sync and one-click Vercel deployment.
target_audience: Developers, indie hackers, and technical founders who want to ship web applications and landing pages rapidly without scaffolding boilerplate.

pricing.price_anchoring: No price shown on page; anchoring deferred to /pricing. Student and Enterprise tiers signal breadth of audience without anchoring cost expectations.

features: Sync with a GitHub repo and push code directly
Visual design mode for fine-tuning with live preview
Agentic workflow: plans tasks, connects to databases, calls APIs autonomously
Design system creation: colors, typography, reusable styles across projects
differentiators: Agentic by default — v0 plans, creates tasks, and connects to databases as it builds
Go from idea to production in seconds with smart, secure infrastructure
Automatic integration with apps and APIs, no accounts required
iOS app for building on the go
trust_signals: Template popularity counts (view and fork metrics visible on all templates)

design_patterns.layout: Centered hero with prompt input, followed by a horizontally scrollable template gallery grid, then a vertical feature list, an agentic workflow diagram section, and a bottom CTA
design_patterns.color_scheme: Predominantly dark/neutral with high-contrast white text; minimal accent colors borrowed from Vercel's brand palette
design_patterns.typography: Clean sans-serif; large, bold headline weights for section titles; matches Vercel's design system
notable_patterns: Prompt input as hero centerpiece (product-led hero)
Template card grid with social proof via view/fork counts
Agentic pipeline diagram (Web → Plan → DB → API → Deploy → LLM)

key_objections_addressed: Speed: 'minutes' and 'seconds' language throughout counters perception that AI tools are slow or iterative
Lock-in: GitHub sync and code ownership signals escape hatch from the platform
Mobile/on-the-go use: iOS app addresses the objection that it's desktop-only
Enterprise viability: explicit Enterprise nav item and 'secure infrastructure' copy
missing_or_weak: No customer logos, testimonials, or named social proof — trust is entirely brand-dependent on Vercel reputation
No pricing transparency on the page itself; friction for users evaluating cost
No demo video or animated product walkthrough in the visible text content
No explicit mention of supported frameworks, languages, or tech stack compatibility
No guarantee, refund policy, or risk-reversal language
Template metrics (views/forks) are anonymous popularity signals, not customer success stories
standout_elements: Product-led hero: the prompt input IS the CTA, removing the need for a separate button
Template gallery with quantified popularity provides immediate social proof of community adoption
Agentic positioning (plan → DB → deploy pipeline) is a strong differentiator claim in the current AI tool landscape
Breadth of audience coverage: Students, Enterprise, iOS all called out in nav, signaling a mature product
Tight benefit copy: 'minutes' and 'seconds' in the subheadline set a concrete speed expectation
